sentences.zh: 首页图片由UMI提供

blocks.html_en: By John Noble Wilford

sentences.en: By John Noble Wilford
sentences.zh: 作者：约翰·诺贝尔·威尔福德

blocks.html_en: Special to The New York Times

sentences.en: Special to The New York Times
sentences.zh: 纽约时报特别报道

blocks.html_en: Voice From Moon: 'Eagle Has Landed'

sentences.en: Voice From Moon: 'Eagle Has Landed'
sentences.zh: 来自月球的声音：“鹰已着陆”

blocks.html_en: Voyage To The Moon: By Archibald MacLeish

sentences.en: Voyage To The Moon: By Archibald MacLeish
sentences.zh: 月球之旅：阿奇博尔德·麦克利什

blocks.html_en: Houston, Monday, July 21--Men have landed and walked on the moon.

sentences.en: Houston, Monday, July 21--Men have landed and walked on the moon.
sentences.zh: 休斯敦，7 月 21 日星期一——人类已经登陆月球并在月球上行走。

blocks.html_en: Two Americans, astronauts of Apollo 11, steered their fragile four-legged lunar module safely and smoothly to the historic landing yesterday at 4:17:40 P.M., Eastern daylight time.

sentences.en: Two Americans, astronauts of Apollo 11, steered their fragile four-legged lunar module safely and smoothly to the historic landing yesterday at 4:17:40 P.M., Eastern daylight time.
sentences.zh: 昨天东部夏令时间下午 4:17:40，两名美国阿波罗 11 号宇航员安全平稳地驾驶着他们脆弱的四足登月舱，实现了历史性的着陆。

blocks.html_en: Neil A. Armstrong, the 38-year-old civilian commander, radioed to earth and the mission control room here:

sentences.en: Neil A.
sentences.zh: 尼尔·A.

sentences.en: Armstrong, the 38-year-old civilian commander, radioed to earth and the mission control room here:
sentences.zh: 38 岁的文职指挥官阿姆斯特朗通过无线电向地球和任务控制室发送了以下信息：

blocks.html_en: "Houston, Tranquility Base here. The Eagle has landed."

sentences.en: "Houston, Tranquility Base here.
sentences.zh: “休斯顿，这里是宁静基地。

sentences.en: The Eagle has landed."
sentences.zh: 老鹰已经着陆了。”

blocks.html_en: The first men to reach the moon--Mr. Armstrong and his co-pilot, Col. Edwin E. Aldrin, Jr. of the Air Force--brought their ship to rest on a level, rock-strewn plain near the southwestern shore of the arid Sea of Tranquility.

sentences.en: The first men to reach the moon--Mr. Armstrong and his co-pilot, Col.
sentences.zh: 第一个登上月球的人——阿姆斯特朗和他的副驾驶上校。

sentences.en: Edwin E.
sentences.zh: 埃德温·E.

sentences.en: Aldrin, Jr. of the Air Force--brought their ship to rest on a level, rock-strewn plain near the southwestern shore of the arid Sea of Tranquility.
sentences.zh: 空军的小奥尔德林将他们的船停在干旱的静海西南岸附近一片岩石遍布的平坦平原上。

blocks.html_en: About six and a half hours later, Mr. Armstrong opened the landing craft's hatch, stepped slowly down the ladder and declared as he planted the first human footprint on the lunar crust:

sentences.en: About six and a half hours later, Mr. Armstrong opened the landing craft's hatch, stepped slowly down the ladder and declared as he planted the first human footprint on the lunar crust:
sentences.zh: 大约六个半小时后，阿姆斯特朗先生打开登陆艇的舱门，慢慢走下梯子，在月壳上留下第一个人类足迹时宣布：

blocks.html_en: "That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind."

sentences.en: "That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind."
sentences.zh: “这是我个人的一小步，却是人类的一大步。”

blocks.html_en: His first step on the moon came at 10:56:20 P.M., as a television camera outside the craft transmitted his every move to an awed and excited audience of hundreds of millions of people on earth.

sentences.en: His first step on the moon came at 10:56:20 P.M., as a television camera outside the craft transmitted his every move to an awed and excited audience of hundreds of millions of people on earth.
sentences.zh: 他在晚上 10:56:20 迈出了月球的第一步，飞船外的电视摄像机将他的一举一动传送给了地球上数亿敬畏而兴奋的观众。

blocks.html_en: Tentative Steps Test Soil

sentences.en: Tentative Steps Test Soil
sentences.zh: 暂定步骤测试土壤

blocks.html_en: Mr. Armstrong's initial steps were tentative tests of the lunar soil's firmness and of his ability to move about easily in his bulky white spacesuit and backpacks and under the influence of lunar gravity, which is one-sixth that of the earth.

sentences.en: Mr. Armstrong's initial steps were tentative tests of the lunar soil's firmness and of his ability to move about easily in his bulky white spacesuit and backpacks and under the influence of lunar gravity, which is one-sixth that of the earth.
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗的最初步骤是试探性测试月球土壤的硬度，以及他穿着笨重的白色宇航服和背包，在月球重力（地球重力的六分之一）的影响下轻松移动的能力。

blocks.html_en: "The surface is fine and powdery," the astronaut reported. "I can pick it up loosely with my toe. It does adhere in fine layers like powdered charcoal to the sole and sides of my boots. I only go in a small fraction of an inch, maybe an eighth of an inch. But I can see the footprints of my boots in the treads in the fine sandy particles.

sentences.en: "The surface is fine and powdery," the astronaut reported.
sentences.zh: “表面很细，呈粉状，”宇航员报告道。

sentences.en: "I can pick it up loosely with my toe.
sentences.zh: “我可以用脚趾松松地捡起它。

sentences.en: It does adhere in fine layers like powdered charcoal to the sole and sides of my boots.
sentences.zh: 它确实像木炭粉一样细密地粘附在我靴子的鞋底和侧面。

sentences.en: I only go in a small fraction of an inch, maybe an eighth of an inch.
sentences.zh: 我只进入了一小部分英寸，也许是八分之一英寸。

sentences.en: But I can see the footprints of my boots in the treads in the fine sandy particles.
sentences.zh: 但我可以在细沙颗粒的胎面中看到靴子的脚印。

blocks.html_en: After 19 minutes of Mr. Armstrong's testing, Colonel Aldrin joined him outside the craft.

sentences.en: After 19 minutes of Mr. Armstrong's testing, Colonel Aldrin joined him outside the craft.
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗先生进行了 19 分钟的测试后，奥尔德林上校在飞船外加入了他。

blocks.html_en: The two men got busy setting up another television camera out from the lunar module, planting an American flag into the ground, scooping up soil and rock samples, deploying scientific experiments and hopping and loping about in a demonstration of their lunar agility.

sentences.en: The two men got busy setting up another television camera out from the lunar module, planting an American flag into the ground, scooping up soil and rock samples, deploying scientific experiments and hopping and loping about in a demonstration of their lunar agility.
sentences.zh: 两人忙着从登月舱中安装另一台电视摄像机，将一面美国国旗插在地上，舀起土壤和岩石样本，进行科学实验，并在月球上跳跃和大步走来展示他们的敏捷性。

blocks.html_en: They found walking and working on the moon less taxing than had been forecast. Mr. Armstrong once reported he was "very comfortable."

sentences.en: They found walking and working on the moon less taxing than had been forecast.
sentences.zh: 他们发现在月球上行走和工作比预期的要轻松得多。

sentences.en: Mr. Armstrong once reported he was "very comfortable."
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗先生曾表示他“非常舒服”。

blocks.html_en: And people back on earth found the black-and-white television pictures of the bug- shaped lunar module and the men tramping about it so sharp and clear as to seem unreal, more like a toy and toy-like figures than human beings on the most daring and far- reaching expedition thus far undertaken.

sentences.en: And people back on earth found the black-and-white television pictures of the bug- shaped lunar module and the men tramping about it so sharp and clear as to seem unreal, more like a toy and toy-like figures than human beings on the most daring and far- reaching expedition thus far undertaken.
sentences.zh: 回到地球的人们发现，虫形登月舱和在其上行走的人们的黑白电视画面如此清晰，以至于看起来不真实，更像是一个玩具和玩具般的人物，而不是迄今为止进行的最大胆和影响最深远的探险中的人类。

blocks.html_en: Nixon Telephones Congratulations

sentences.en: Nixon Telephones Congratulations
sentences.zh: 尼克松打电话祝贺

blocks.html_en: During one break in the astronauts' work, President Nixon congratulated them from the White House in what, he said, "certainly has to be the most historic telephone call ever made."

sentences.en: During one break in the astronauts' work, President Nixon congratulated them from the White House in what, he said, "certainly has to be the most historic telephone call ever made."
sentences.zh: 在宇航员工作休息期间，尼克松总统在白宫向他们表示祝贺，他说，“这无疑是有史以来最具历史意义的电话”。

blocks.html_en: "Because of what you have done," the President told the astronauts, "the heavens have become a part of man's world. And as you talk to us from the Sea of Tranquility it required us to redouble our efforts to bring peace and tranquility to earth.

sentences.en: "Because of what you have done," the President told the astronauts, "the heavens have become a part of man's world.
sentences.zh: 总统对宇航员说：“由于你们所做的一切，天空已成为人类世界的一部分。

sentences.en: And as you talk to us from the Sea of Tranquility it required us to redouble our efforts to bring peace and tranquility to earth.
sentences.zh: 当您在宁静之海与我们交谈时，我们需要加倍努力，为地球带来和平与安宁。

blocks.html_en: "For one priceless moment in the whole history of man all the people on this earth are truly one--one in their pride in what you have done and one in our prayers that you will return safely to earth."

sentences.en: "For one priceless moment in the whole history of man all the people on this earth are truly one--one in their pride in what you have done and one in our prayers that you will return safely to earth."
sentences.zh: “这是人类整个历史上一个无价的时刻，地球上的所有人都真正团结在一起——他们为你们所做的一切感到自豪，我们祈祷你们能安全返回地球。”

blocks.html_en: Mr. Armstrong replied:

sentences.en: Mr. Armstrong replied:
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗先生回答说：

blocks.html_en: "Thank you Mr. President. It's a great honor and privilege for us to be here representing not only the United States but men of peace of all nations, men with interests and a curiosity and men with a vision for the future."

sentences.en: "Thank you Mr. President.
sentences.zh: “谢谢总统先生。

sentences.en: It's a great honor and privilege for us to be here representing not only the United States but men of peace of all nations, men with interests and a curiosity and men with a vision for the future."
sentences.zh: 我们非常荣幸能够在这里不仅代表美国，还代表所有国家的和平人士、有兴趣和好奇心的人士以及对未来有远见的人士。”

blocks.html_en: Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin returned to their landing craft and closed the hatch at 1:12 A.M., 2 hours 21 minutes after opening the hatch on the moon. While the third member of the crew, Lieut. Col. Michael Collins of the Air Force, kept his orbital vigil overhead in the command ship, the two moon explorers settled down to sleep.

sentences.en: Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin returned to their landing craft and closed the hatch at 1:12 A.M., 2 hours 21 minutes after opening the hatch on the moon.
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗先生和奥尔德林上校返回登陆艇，并于凌晨 1 点 12 分关闭了舱门，即在月球上打开舱门 2 小时 21 分钟后。

sentences.en: While the third member of the crew, Lieut.
sentences.zh: 而第三名船员，中尉。

sentences.en: Col.
sentences.zh: 上校

sentences.en: Michael Collins of the Air Force, kept his orbital vigil overhead in the command ship, the two moon explorers settled down to sleep.
sentences.zh: 空军的迈克尔·柯林斯在指挥舰上空进行轨道守夜，两位月球探险家坐下来睡觉。

blocks.html_en: Outside their vehicle the astronauts had found a bleak world. It was just before dawn, with the sun low over the eastern horizon behind them and the chill of the long lunar nights still clinging to the boulders, small craters and hills before them.

sentences.en: Outside their vehicle the astronauts had found a bleak world.
sentences.zh: 在他们的飞行器外面，宇航员发现了一个荒凉的世界。

sentences.en: It was just before dawn, with the sun low over the eastern horizon behind them and the chill of the long lunar nights still clinging to the boulders, small craters and hills before them.
sentences.zh: 黎明前夕，太阳在他们身后的东方地平线上低垂，漫长的月夜的寒冷仍然附着在他们面前的巨石、小陨石坑和山丘上。

blocks.html_en: Colonel Aldrin said that he could see "literally thousands of small craters" and a low hill out in the distance. But most of all he was impressed initially by the "variety of shapes, angularities, granularities" of the rocks and soil where the landing craft, code-named Eagle had set down.

sentences.en: Colonel Aldrin said that he could see "literally thousands of small craters" and a low hill out in the distance.
sentences.zh: 奥尔德林上校说，他可以看到“实际上有数千个小陨石坑”，远处还有一座低矮的山丘。

sentences.en: But most of all he was impressed initially by the "variety of shapes, angularities, granularities" of the rocks and soil where the landing craft, code-named Eagle had set down.
sentences.zh: 但最重要的是，代号为“鹰”的登陆艇最初降落的岩石和土壤的“各种形状、棱角和颗粒度”给他留下了深刻的印象。

blocks.html_en: The landing was made four miles west of the aiming point, but well within the designated area. An apparent error in some data fed into the craft's guidance computer from the earth was said to have accounted for the discrepancy.

sentences.en: The landing was made four miles west of the aiming point, but well within the designated area.
sentences.zh: 着陆地点在瞄准点以西四英里处，但完全在指定区域内。

sentences.en: An apparent error in some data fed into the craft's guidance computer from the earth was said to have accounted for the discrepancy.
sentences.zh: 据说，从地球输入飞行器制导计算机的一些数据存在明显错误，这是造成这种差异的原因。

blocks.html_en: Suddenly the astronauts were startled to see that the computer was guiding them toward a possibly disastrous touchdown in a boulder-filled crater about the size of a football field.

sentences.en: Suddenly the astronauts were startled to see that the computer was guiding them toward a possibly disastrous touchdown in a boulder-filled crater about the size of a football field.
sentences.zh: 突然，宇航员们惊讶地发现，计算机正引导他们在一个足球场大小、布满巨石的陨石坑中进行可能是灾难性的着陆。

blocks.html_en: Mr. Armstrong grabbed manual control of the vehicle and guided it safely over the crater to a smoother spot, the rocket engine stirring a cloud of moon dust during the final seconds of descent.

sentences.en: Mr. Armstrong grabbed manual control of the vehicle and guided it safely over the crater to a smoother spot, the rocket engine stirring a cloud of moon dust during the final seconds of descent.
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗先生手动控制了飞行器，引导它安全越过陨石坑到达更平坦的地方，火箭发动机在下降的最后几秒钟搅动了一团月球尘埃。

blocks.html_en: Soon after the landing, upon checking and finding the spacecraft in good condition, Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin made their decision to open the hatch and get out earlier than originally scheduled. The flight plan had called for the moon walk to begin at 2:12 A.M.

sentences.en: Soon after the landing, upon checking and finding the spacecraft in good condition, Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin made their decision to open the hatch and get out earlier than originally scheduled.
sentences.zh: 着陆后不久，在检查并发现航天器状况良好后，阿姆斯特朗先生和奥尔德林上校决定打开舱门并比原计划提前离开。

sentences.en: The flight plan had called for the moon walk to begin at 2:12 A.M.
sentences.zh: 飞行计划要求月球漫步于凌晨 2 点 12 分开始。

blocks.html_en: Flight controllers here said that the early moon walk would not mean that the astronauts would also leave the moon earlier. The lift-off is scheduled to come at about 1:55 P.M. today.

sentences.en: Flight controllers here said that the early moon walk would not mean that the astronauts would also leave the moon earlier.
sentences.zh: 这里的飞行控制人员表示，提前进行月球行走并不意味着宇航员也会提前离开月球。

sentences.en: The lift-off is scheduled to come at about 1:55 P.M.
sentences.zh: 起飞时间定于下午 1:55 左右。

sentences.en: today.
sentences.zh: 今天。

blocks.html_en: Their departure from the landing craft out onto the surface was delayed for a time when they had trouble depressurizing the cabin so that they could open the hatch. All the oxygen in the cabin had to be vented.

sentences.en: Their departure from the landing craft out onto the surface was delayed for a time when they had trouble depressurizing the cabin so that they could open the hatch.
sentences.zh: 他们从登陆艇出发到地面的时间被推迟了一段时间，因为他们无法为舱室减压以便打开舱门。

sentences.en: All the oxygen in the cabin had to be vented.
sentences.zh: 机舱内的氧气必须全部排出。

blocks.html_en: Once the pressure gauge finally dropped to zero, they opened the hatch and Mr. Armstrong stepped out on the small porch at the top of the nine-step ladder.

sentences.en: Once the pressure gauge finally dropped to zero, they opened the hatch and Mr. Armstrong stepped out on the small porch at the top of the nine-step ladder.
sentences.zh: 当压力表最终降至零时，他们打开舱门，阿姆斯特朗先生走出九级梯顶部的小门廊。

blocks.html_en: "O.K., Houston, I'm on the porch," he reported, as he descended.

sentences.en: "O.K., Houston, I'm on the porch," he reported, as he descended.
sentences.zh: “好的，休斯顿，我在门廊上，”他一边下楼一边报告道。

blocks.html_en: On the second step from the top, he pulled a lanyard that released a fold-down equipment compartment on the side of the lunar module. This deployed the television camera that transmitted the dramatic pictures of man's first steps on the moon.

sentences.en: On the second step from the top, he pulled a lanyard that released a fold-down equipment compartment on the side of the lunar module.
sentences.zh: 在从顶部开始的第二级台阶上，他拉动一根系索，将登月舱侧面的折叠式设备舱释放出来。

sentences.en: This deployed the television camera that transmitted the dramatic pictures of man's first steps on the moon.
sentences.zh: 这部署了电视摄像机，传输人类首次登上月球的戏剧性画面。

blocks.html_en: Ancient Dream Fulfilled

sentences.en: Ancient Dream Fulfilled
sentences.zh: 古老的梦想实现了

blocks.html_en: It was man's first landing on another world, the realization of centuries of dreams, the fulfillment of a decade of striving, a triumph of modern technology and personal courage, the most dramatic demonstration of what man can do if he applies his mind and resources with single-minded determination.

sentences.en: It was man's first landing on another world, the realization of centuries of dreams, the fulfillment of a decade of striving, a triumph of modern technology and personal courage, the most dramatic demonstration of what man can do if he applies his mind and resources with single-minded determination.
sentences.zh: 这是人类第一次登陆另一个世界，实现了几个世纪的梦想，完成了十年的奋斗，是现代技术和个人勇气的胜利，最戏剧性地展示了如果人类一心一意地运用自己的思想和资源，就能取得什么成就。

blocks.html_en: The moon, long the symbol of the impossible and the inaccessible, was now within man's reach, the first port of call in this new age of spacefaring.

sentences.en: The moon, long the symbol of the impossible and the inaccessible, was now within man's reach, the first port of call in this new age of spacefaring.
sentences.zh: 月球长期以来一直是不可能和难以到达的象征，现在人类已经触手可及，成为航天新时代的第一个停靠港。

blocks.html_en: Immediately after the landing, Dr. Thomas O. Paine, administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, telephoned President Nixon in Washington to report:

sentences.en: Immediately after the landing, Dr. Thomas O.
sentences.zh: 着陆后，托马斯·O.

sentences.en: Paine, administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, telephoned President Nixon in Washington to report:
sentences.zh: 美国国家航空航天局局长潘恩致电华盛顿的尼克松总统报告：

blocks.html_en: "Mr. President, it is my honor on behalf of the entire NASA team to report to you that the Eagle has landed on the Sea of Tranquility and our astronauts are safe and looking forward to starting the exploration of the moon."

sentences.en: "Mr. President, it is my honor on behalf of the entire NASA team to report to you that the Eagle has landed on the Sea of Tranquility and our astronauts are safe and looking forward to starting the exploration of the moon."
sentences.zh: “总统先生，我很荣幸代表整个 NASA 团队向您报告，鹰号已经降落在宁静海，我们的宇航员很安全，并期待着开始月球探索。”

blocks.html_en: The landing craft from the Apollo 11 spaceship was scheduled to remain on the moon about 22 hours, while Colonel Collins of the Air Force, the third member of the Apollo 11 crew, piloted the command ship, Columbia, in orbit overhead.

sentences.en: The landing craft from the Apollo 11 spaceship was scheduled to remain on the moon about 22 hours, while Colonel Collins of the Air Force, the third member of the Apollo 11 crew, piloted the command ship, Columbia, in orbit overhead.
sentences.zh: 阿波罗 11 号飞船的登陆艇计划在月球上停留约 22 小时，而阿波罗 11 号机组的第三名成员、空军上校柯林斯则驾驶指挥舰哥伦比亚号在上空轨道上飞行。

blocks.html_en: "You're looking good in every respect," Mission Control told the two men of Eagle after examining data indicating that the module should be able to remain on the moon the full 22 hours.

sentences.en: "You're looking good in every respect," Mission Control told the two men of Eagle after examining data indicating that the module should be able to remain on the moon the full 22 hours.
sentences.zh: “你们在各方面看起来都很好，”任务控制中心在检查了表明该模块应该能够在月球上停留整整 22 小时的数据后告诉 Eagle 的两名人员。

blocks.html_en: Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin planned to sleep after the moon walk and then make their preparations for the lift-off for the return to a rendezvous with Colonel Collins in the command ship.

sentences.en: Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin planned to sleep after the moon walk and then make their preparations for the lift-off for the return to a rendezvous with Colonel Collins in the command ship.
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗先生和奥尔德林上校计划在月球漫步后睡觉，然后为升空做准备，以便返回与指挥舰上的柯林斯上校会合。

blocks.html_en: Apollo 11's journey into history began last Wednesday from launching pad 39-A at Cape Kennedy, Fla. After an almost flawless three-day flight, the joined command ship and lunar module swept into an orbit of the moon yesterday afternoon.

sentences.en: Apollo 11's journey into history began last Wednesday from launching pad 39-A at Cape Kennedy, Fla.
sentences.zh: 阿波罗 11 号的历史之旅于上周三从佛罗里达州肯尼迪角的 39-A 发射台开始。

sentences.en: After an almost flawless three-day flight, the joined command ship and lunar module swept into an orbit of the moon yesterday afternoon.
sentences.zh: 经过几乎完美的三天飞行后，联合指挥船和登月舱于昨天下午进入月球轨道。

blocks.html_en: The three men were awake for their big day at 7 A.M. when their spacecraft emerged from behind the moon on its 10th revolution, moving from east to west across the face of the moon along its equator.

sentences.en: The three men were awake for their big day at 7 A.M.
sentences.zh: 早上 7 点，三个人就起床迎接他们的大日子了。

sentences.en: when their spacecraft emerged from behind the moon on its 10th revolution, moving from east to west across the face of the moon along its equator.
sentences.zh: 当他们的航天器在第十次公转时从月球后面出现，沿着月球赤道从东向西移动穿过月球表面。

blocks.html_en: Their orbit was 73.6 miles by 64 miles in altitude, their speed 3,660 miles an hour. At that altitude and speed, it took about two hours to complete a full orbit of the moon.

sentences.en: Their orbit was 73.6 miles by 64 miles in altitude, their speed 3,660 miles an hour.
sentences.zh: 它们的轨道高度为 73.6 英里 x 64 英里，速度为每小时 3,660 英里。

sentences.en: At that altitude and speed, it took about two hours to complete a full orbit of the moon.
sentences.zh: 以这样的高度和速度，绕月球一周大约需要两个小时。

blocks.html_en: The sun was rising over their landing site on the Sea of Tranquility.

sentences.en: The sun was rising over their landing site on the Sea of Tranquility.
sentences.zh: 太阳正在他们在宁静海的着陆点升起。

blocks.html_en: "We can pick out almost all of the features we've identified previously," Mr. Armstrong reported.

sentences.en: "We can pick out almost all of the features we've identified previously," Mr. Armstrong reported.
sentences.zh: “我们可以挑出几乎所有我们之前发现的特征，”阿姆斯特朗先生报告说。

blocks.html_en: After breakfast, on their 11th revolution Colonel Aldrin and then Mr. Armstrong, both dressed in their white pressurized suits, crawled through the connecting tunnel into the lunar module.

sentences.en: After breakfast, on their 11th revolution Colonel Aldrin and then Mr. Armstrong, both dressed in their white pressurized suits, crawled through the connecting tunnel into the lunar module.
sentences.zh: 早餐后，奥尔德林上校和阿姆斯特朗先生进行了第十一次革命，两人都穿着白色加压服，爬过连接隧道进入登月舱。

blocks.html_en: They turned on the electrical power, checked all the switch settings on the cockpit panel and checked communications with the command ship and the ground controllers. Everything was "nominal," as the spacemen say.

sentences.en: They turned on the electrical power, checked all the switch settings on the cockpit panel and checked communications with the command ship and the ground controllers.
sentences.zh: 他们打开电源，检查驾驶舱面板上的所有开关设置，并检查与指挥舰和地面控制器的通信。

sentences.en: Everything was "nominal," as the spacemen say.
sentences.zh: 正如太空人所说，一切都是“名义上的”。

blocks.html_en: LM Ready for Descent

sentences.en: LM Ready for Descent
sentences.zh: 登月舱准备下降

blocks.html_en: The lunar module was ready. Its four legs with yard-wide footpads were extended so that the height of the 16-ton vehicle now measured 22 feet and 11 inches and its width 31 feet.

sentences.en: The lunar module was ready.
sentences.zh: 登月舱已准备就绪。

sentences.en: Its four legs with yard-wide footpads were extended so that the height of the 16-ton vehicle now measured 22 feet and 11 inches and its width 31 feet.
sentences.zh: 它的四条腿和一码宽的脚垫被延长，因此这辆重 16 吨的车辆现在的高度为 22 英尺 11 英寸，宽度为 31 英尺。

blocks.html_en: Mr. Armstrong stood at the left side of the cockpit, and Colonel Aldrin at the right. Both were loosely restrained by harnesses. They had closed the hatch to the connecting tunnel.

sentences.en: Mr. Armstrong stood at the left side of the cockpit, and Colonel Aldrin at the right.
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗先生站在驾驶舱左侧，奥尔德林上校站在右侧。

sentences.en: Both were loosely restrained by harnesses.
sentences.zh: 两人都被安全带松松地束缚着。

sentences.en: They had closed the hatch to the connecting tunnel.
sentences.zh: 他们关闭了连接隧道的舱门。

blocks.html_en: The walls of their craft were finely milled aluminum foil. If anything happened so that it could not return to the command ship, the lunar module would be too delicate to withstand a plunge through earth's atmosphere, even if it had the rocket power.

sentences.en: The walls of their craft were finely milled aluminum foil.
sentences.zh: 他们的工艺壁是经过精细研磨的铝箔。

sentences.en: If anything happened so that it could not return to the command ship, the lunar module would be too delicate to withstand a plunge through earth's atmosphere, even if it had the rocket power.
sentences.zh: 如果发生任何事情导致它无法返回指挥船，登月舱就会太脆弱，无法承受冲入地球大气层，即使它有火箭动力。

blocks.html_en: Nearly three-fourths of the vehicle's weight was in propellants for the descent and ascent rockets--Aerozine 50 and nitrogen oxide, which substituted for the oxygen, making combustion possible.

sentences.en: Nearly three-fourths of the vehicle's weight was in propellants for the descent and ascent rockets--Aerozine 50 and nitrogen oxide, which substituted for the oxygen, making combustion possible.
sentences.zh: 飞行器重量的近四分之三用于下降和上升火箭的推进剂——Aerozine 50 和氧化氮，它们取代了氧气，使燃烧成为可能。

blocks.html_en: It was an ungainly craft that creaked and groaned in flight. But years of development and testing had determined that it was the lightest and most practical way to get two men to the moon's surface.

sentences.en: It was an ungainly craft that creaked and groaned in flight.
sentences.zh: 这是一艘笨拙的飞行器，在飞行中吱吱作响。

sentences.en: But years of development and testing had determined that it was the lightest and most practical way to get two men to the moon's surface.
sentences.zh: 但多年的开发和测试已经确定，这是让两个人登上月球表面的最轻、最实用的方法。

blocks.html_en: Before Apollo 11 disappeared behind the moon near the end of its 12th orbit, mission control gave the astronauts their "go" for undocking--the separation of Eagle from Columbia.

sentences.en: Before Apollo 11 disappeared behind the moon near the end of its 12th orbit, mission control gave the astronauts their "go" for undocking--the separation of Eagle from Columbia.
sentences.zh: 在阿波罗 11 号在第 12 条轨道即将结束时消失在月球后面之前，任务控制中心向宇航员发出了脱离对接的指令——即鹰号与哥伦比亚号分离。

blocks.html_en: Colonel Collins had already released 12 of the latches holding the two ships together at the connecting tunnel. He did this when he closed the hatch at the command ship's nose. While behind the moon, he was to flip a switch on the control panel to release the three remaining latches by a spring action.

sentences.en: Colonel Collins had already released 12 of the latches holding the two ships together at the connecting tunnel.
sentences.zh: 柯林斯上校已经松开了连接隧道处将两艘船固定在一起的 12 个闩锁。

sentences.en: He did this when he closed the hatch at the command ship's nose.
sentences.zh: 当他关闭指挥舰机头的舱门时，他做到了这一点。

sentences.en: While behind the moon, he was to flip a switch on the control panel to release the three remaining latches by a spring action.
sentences.zh: 在月球后面时，他要按下控制面板上的开关，通过弹簧动作释放剩余的三个闩锁。

blocks.html_en: At 1:50 P.M., when communications signals were reacquired, Mission Control asked: "How does it look?"

sentences.en: At 1:50 P.M., when communications signals were reacquired, Mission Control asked: "How does it look?"
sentences.zh: 下午 1 点 50 分，当重新获取通信信号时，任务控制中心问道：“看起来怎么样？”

blocks.html_en: "Eagle has wings," Mr. Armstrong replied.

sentences.en: "Eagle has wings," Mr. Armstrong replied.
sentences.zh: “鹰有翅膀，”阿姆斯特朗先生回答道。

blocks.html_en: The two ships were then only a few feet apart. But at 2:12 P.M., Colonel Collins fired the command ship's maneuvering rockets to move about two miles away and in a slightly different orbit from the lunar module.

sentences.en: The two ships were then only a few feet apart.
sentences.zh: 两艘船当时相距只有几英尺。

sentences.en: But at 2:12 P.M., Colonel Collins fired the command ship's maneuvering rockets to move about two miles away and in a slightly different orbit from the lunar module.
sentences.zh: 但下午 2 点 12 分，柯林斯上校发射了指挥舰的机动火箭，移动到距离登月舱约两英里的地方，轨道与登月舱略有不同。

blocks.html_en: "It looks like you've got a fine-looking flying machine there, Eagle, despite the fact you're upside down," Colonel Collins commented, watching the spidery lunar module receding in the distance.

sentences.en: "It looks like you've got a fine-looking flying machine there, Eagle, despite the fact you're upside down," Colonel Collins commented, watching the spidery lunar module receding in the distance.
sentences.zh: “看起来你那里有一架漂亮的飞行器，老鹰，尽管你实际上是颠倒的，”柯林斯上校评论道，看着远处的蜘蛛状登月舱。

blocks.html_en: "Somebody's upside down," Mr. Armstrong replied.

sentences.en: "Somebody's upside down," Mr. Armstrong replied.
sentences.zh: “有人颠倒了，”阿姆斯特朗先生回答道。

blocks.html_en: What is "up" and what is "down" is never quite clear in the absence of landmarks and the sensation of gravity's pull.

sentences.en: What is "up" and what is "down" is never quite clear in the absence of landmarks and the sensation of gravity's pull.
sentences.zh: 在没有地标和重力感觉的情况下，什么是“上”、什么是“下”永远不会很清楚。

blocks.html_en: As Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin rode the lunar module back around to the moon's far side, the rocket engine in the vehicle's lower stage was pointed toward the line of flight. The two pilots were leaning toward the cockpit controls, riding backwards and facing downward.

sentences.en: As Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin rode the lunar module back around to the moon's far side, the rocket engine in the vehicle's lower stage was pointed toward the line of flight.
sentences.zh: 当阿姆斯特朗先生和奥尔德林上校乘坐登月舱返回月球背面时，飞行器下级的火箭发动机指向飞行线。

sentences.en: The two pilots were leaning toward the cockpit controls, riding backwards and facing downward.
sentences.zh: 两名飞行员身体朝驾驶舱控制装置倾斜，面朝下向后行驶。

blocks.html_en: "Everything is 'go,'" they were assured by Mission Control.

sentences.en: "Everything is 'go,'" they were assured by Mission Control.
sentences.zh: 任务控制中心向他们保证：“一切都在‘进行’。”

blocks.html_en: Their on-board guidance and navigation computer was instructed to trigger a 29.8-second firing of the descent rocket, the 9,870-pound-thrust throttable engine that would slow down the lunar module and send it toward the moon on a long, curving trajectory.

sentences.en: Their on-board guidance and navigation computer was instructed to trigger a 29.8-second firing of the descent rocket, the 9,870-pound-thrust throttable engine that would slow down the lunar module and send it toward the moon on a long, curving trajectory.
sentences.zh: 他们的机载制导和导航计算机接到指示，触发下降火箭在 29.8 秒内发射，这是一种推力为 9,870 磅的节流发动机，可以减慢登月舱的速度，并沿着一条长而弯曲的轨道将其送向月球。

blocks.html_en: The firing was set to take place at 3:08 P.M., when the craft would be behind the moon and once again out of touch with the ground.

sentences.en: The firing was set to take place at 3:08 P.M., when the craft would be behind the moon and once again out of touch with the ground.
sentences.zh: 发射定于下午 3 点 08 分进行，届时飞船将位于月球后面并再次与地面脱离。

blocks.html_en: Suspense built up in the control room here. Flight controllers stood silently at their consoles. Among those waiting for word of the rocket firing were Dr. Thomas O. Paine, the space agency's administrator, most of the Apollo project officials and several astronauts.

sentences.en: Suspense built up in the control room here.
sentences.zh: 这里的控制室里充满了悬念。

sentences.en: Flight controllers stood silently at their consoles.
sentences.zh: 飞行控制员静静地站在控制台前。

sentences.en: Among those waiting for word of the rocket firing were Dr. Thomas O.
sentences.zh: 托马斯·O·博士 (Dr. Thomas O.

sentences.en: Paine, the space agency's administrator, most of the Apollo project officials and several astronauts.
sentences.zh: 航天局局长潘恩、阿波罗计划的大部分官员和几名宇航员。

blocks.html_en: At 3:46 P.M., contact was established with the command ship.

sentences.en: At 3:46 P.M., contact was established with the command ship.
sentences.zh: 下午3点46分，与指挥舰建立了联系。

blocks.html_en: Colonel Collins reported, "Listen, baby, things are going just swimmingly, just beautiful."

sentences.en: Colonel Collins reported, "Listen, baby, things are going just swimmingly, just beautiful."
sentences.zh: 柯林斯上校报告说：“听着，宝贝，一切进展顺利，非常美好。”

blocks.html_en: There was still no word from the lunar module for two minutes. Then came a weak signal, some static and whistling, and finally the calm voice of Mr. Armstrong.

sentences.en: There was still no word from the lunar module for two minutes.
sentences.zh: 两分钟后，登月舱仍然没有任何消息。

sentences.en: Then came a weak signal, some static and whistling, and finally the calm voice of Mr. Armstrong.
sentences.zh: 然后传来微弱的信号，有些静电干扰和口哨声，最后是阿姆斯特朗先生平静的声音。

blocks.html_en: "The burn was on time," the Apollo 11 commander declared.

sentences.en: "The burn was on time," the Apollo 11 commander declared.
sentences.zh: “燃烧准时，”阿波罗 11 号指挥官宣称。

blocks.html_en: When he read out data on the beginning of the descent, Mission Control concluded that it "look great." The lunar module had already descended from an altitude of 65.5 miles to 21 miles and was coasting steadily downward.

sentences.en: When he read out data on the beginning of the descent, Mission Control concluded that it "look great."
sentences.zh: 当他读出下降开始时的数据时，任务控制中心得出结论：“看起来很棒”。

sentences.en: The lunar module had already descended from an altitude of 65.5 miles to 21 miles and was coasting steadily downward.
sentences.zh: 登月舱已经从 65.5 英里的高度下降到 21 英里，并且正在稳定地滑行下降。

blocks.html_en: Eugene F. Kranz, the flight director, turned to his associates and said, "We're off to a good start. Play it cool."

sentences.en: Eugene F.
sentences.zh: 尤金·F.

sentences.en: Kranz, the flight director, turned to his associates and said, "We're off to a good start.
sentences.zh: 飞行指挥克兰兹转向他的同事说：“我们有了一个良好的开端。

sentences.en: Play it cool."
sentences.zh: 玩得冷静一点。”

blocks.html_en: Colonel Aldrin reported some oscillations in the vehicle's antenna, but nothing serious. Several times the astronauts were told to turn the vehicle slightly to move the antenna into a better position for communications over the 230,000 miles.

sentences.en: Colonel Aldrin reported some oscillations in the vehicle's antenna, but nothing serious.
sentences.zh: 奥尔德林上校报告说，车辆的天线出现了一些振荡，但并不严重。

sentences.en: Several times the astronauts were told to turn the vehicle slightly to move the antenna into a better position for communications over the 230,000 miles.
sentences.zh: 宇航员多次被告知稍微转动飞行器，将天线移动到一个更好的位置，以便在 230,000 英里的范围内进行通信。

blocks.html_en: "You're 'go' for PDI," radioed Mission Control, referring to the powered descent initiation--the beginning of the nearly 13-minute final blast of the rocket to the soft touchdown.

sentences.en: "You're 'go' for PDI," radioed Mission Control, referring to the powered descent initiation--the beginning of the nearly 13-minute final blast of the rocket to the soft touchdown.
sentences.zh: 任务控制中心通过无线电广播说：“你要‘开始’PDI了。”指的是动力下降启动——火箭最后一次爆炸的近13分钟的开始，直至软着陆。

blocks.html_en: When the two men reached an altitude of 50,000 feet, which was approximately the lowest point reached by Apollo 10 in May, green lights on the computer display keyboard in the cockpit blinked the number 99.

sentences.en: When the two men reached an altitude of 50,000 feet, which was approximately the lowest point reached by Apollo 10 in May, green lights on the computer display keyboard in the cockpit blinked the number 99.
sentences.zh: 当两人到达 50,000 英尺的高度（大约是阿波罗 10 号 5 月份到达的最低点）时，驾驶舱电脑显示屏键盘上的绿灯闪烁着数字 99。

blocks.html_en: This signaled Mr. Armstrong that he had five seconds to decide whether to go ahead for the landing or continue on its orbital path back to the command ship. He pressed the "proceed" button.

sentences.en: This signaled Mr. Armstrong that he had five seconds to decide whether to go ahead for the landing or continue on its orbital path back to the command ship.
sentences.zh: 这向阿姆斯特朗发出信号，他有五秒钟的时间来决定是继续着陆还是继续沿着轨道返回指挥舰。

sentences.en: He pressed the "proceed" button.
sentences.zh: 他按下了“继续”按钮。

blocks.html_en: The throttleable engine built up thrust gradually, firing continuously as the lunar module descended along the steadily steepening trajectory to the landing site about 250 miles away:

sentences.en: The throttleable engine built up thrust gradually, firing continuously as the lunar module descended along the steadily steepening trajectory to the landing site about 250 miles away:
sentences.zh: 当登月舱沿着逐渐陡峭的轨迹下降到大约 250 英里外的着陆点时，节流发动机逐渐建立推力并持续点火：

blocks.html_en: "Looking good," Mission Control radioed the men.

sentences.en: "Looking good," Mission Control radioed the men.
sentences.zh: “看起来不错，”任务控制中心用无线电对这些人说。

blocks.html_en: Four minutes after the firing the lunar module was down to 40,000 feet. After five and a half minutes, it was 33,500 feet. At six minutes, 27,000 feet.

sentences.en: Four minutes after the firing the lunar module was down to 40,000 feet.
sentences.zh: 点火四分钟后，登月舱下降到 40,000 英尺的高度。

sentences.en: After five and a half minutes, it was 33,500 feet.
sentences.zh: 五分半钟后，高度达到了 33,500 英尺。

sentences.en: At six minutes, 27,000 feet.
sentences.zh: 六分钟，海拔 27,000 英尺。

blocks.html_en: "Better than the simulator," said Colonel Aldrin, referring to their practice landings at the spacecraft center.

sentences.en: "Better than the simulator," said Colonel Aldrin, referring to their practice landings at the spacecraft center.
sentences.zh: “比模拟器更好，”奥尔德林上校在谈到他们在航天器中心的着陆练习时说道。

blocks.html_en: Seven minutes after the firing, the men were 21,000 feet above the surface and still moving forward toward the landing site. The guidance computer was driving the rocket engine.

sentences.en: Seven minutes after the firing, the men were 21,000 feet above the surface and still moving forward toward the landing site.
sentences.zh: 射击七分钟后，这些人已经升到离地表 21,000 英尺的高度，并且仍在向着陆点前进。

sentences.en: The guidance computer was driving the rocket engine.
sentences.zh: 制导计算机正在驱动火箭发动机。

blocks.html_en: The lunar module was slowing down. At an altitude of about 7,200 feet, with the landing site still about five miles ahead, the computer commanded control jets to fire and tilt the bug-shaped craft almost upright so that its triangular windows pointed forward.

sentences.en: The lunar module was slowing down.
sentences.zh: 登月舱正在减速。

sentences.en: At an altitude of about 7,200 feet, with the landing site still about five miles ahead, the computer commanded control jets to fire and tilt the bug-shaped craft almost upright so that its triangular windows pointed forward.
sentences.zh: 在大约 7,200 英尺的高度，着陆点仍在前方约 5 英里处，计算机命令控制喷气机发射并将虫形飞行器倾斜到几乎直立，使其三角形窗户指向前方。

blocks.html_en: Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin then got their first close-up view of the plain they were aiming for. It was then about three and a half minutes to touchdown.

sentences.en: Mr. Armstrong and Colonel Aldrin then got their first close-up view of the plain they were aiming for.
sentences.zh: 阿姆斯特朗先生和奥尔德林上校随后第一次近距离观察了他们的目标平原。

sentences.en: It was then about three and a half minutes to touchdown.
sentences.zh: 距离着陆还有大约三分半钟。

blocks.html_en: The brownish-gray panorama rushed below them--myriad craters hills and ridges, deep cracks and ancient rubble on the moon, which Dr. Robert Jastrow, the space agency scientist, called the "Rosetta Stone of life."

sentences.en: The brownish-gray panorama rushed below them--myriad craters hills and ridges, deep cracks and ancient rubble on the moon, which Dr. Robert Jastrow, the space agency scientist, called the "Rosetta Stone of life."
sentences.zh: 棕灰色的全景尽收眼底——月球上无数的陨石坑、山丘和山脊、深深的裂缝和古老的碎石，航天局科学家罗伯特·贾斯特罗博士将其称为“生命的罗塞塔石碑”。

blocks.html_en: "You're 'go' for landing," Mission Control informed the two men.

sentences.en: "You're 'go' for landing," Mission Control informed the two men.
sentences.zh: “你们要‘准备’着陆了，”任务控制中心告诉两人。

blocks.html_en: The Eagle closed in, dropping about 20 feet a second, until it was hovering almost directly over the landing area at an altitude of 500 feet.

sentences.en: The Eagle closed in, dropping about 20 feet a second, until it was hovering almost directly over the landing area at an altitude of 500 feet.
sentences.zh: 鹰号接近，以每秒约 20 英尺的速度下降，直到它几乎直接悬停在高度 500 英尺的着陆区上空。

blocks.html_en: Its floor was littered with boulders.

sentences.en: Its floor was littered with boulders.
sentences.zh: 它的地板上散落着巨石。

blocks.html_en: It was when the craft reached an altitude of 300 feet that Mr. Armstrong took over semimanual control for the rest of the way. The computer continued to have control of the rocket firing, but the astronaut could adjust the craft's hovering position.

sentences.en: It was when the craft reached an altitude of 300 feet that Mr. Armstrong took over semimanual control for the rest of the way.
sentences.zh: 当飞船到达 300 英尺的高度时，阿姆斯特朗先生在剩下的路程中接管了半手动控制。

sentences.en: The computer continued to have control of the rocket firing, but the astronaut could adjust the craft's hovering position.
sentences.zh: 计算机继续控制火箭发射，但宇航员可以调整飞船的悬停位置。

blocks.html_en: He was expected to take over such control anyway, but the sight of a crater looming ahead at the touchdown point made it imperative.

sentences.en: He was expected to take over such control anyway, but the sight of a crater looming ahead at the touchdown point made it imperative.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，他都应该接管这种控制权，但在着陆点前方若隐若现的陨石坑的景象使他势在必行。

blocks.html_en: As Mr. Armstrong said later, "The auto-targeting was taking us right into a football field- sized crater, with a large number of big boulders and rocks."

sentences.en: As Mr. Armstrong said later, "The auto-targeting was taking us right into a football field- sized crater, with a large number of big boulders and rocks."
sentences.zh: 正如阿姆斯特朗先生后来所说，“自动瞄准将我们带入了一个足球场大小的火山口，里面有大量的巨石和岩石。”

blocks.html_en: For about 90 seconds, he peered through the window in search of a clear touchdown point. Using the lever at his right hand, he tilted the vehicle forward to redirect the firing of the maneuvering jets and thus shift its hovering position.

sentences.en: For about 90 seconds, he peered through the window in search of a clear touchdown point.
sentences.zh: 在大约 90 秒的时间里，他透过窗户观察，寻找明显的着陆点。

sentences.en: Using the lever at his right hand, he tilted the vehicle forward to redirect the firing of the maneuvering jets and thus shift its hovering position.
sentences.zh: 他利用右手的操纵杆，将飞行器向前倾斜，以改变机动喷气机的发射方向，从而改变其悬停位置。

blocks.html_en: Finally, Mr. Armstrong found the spot he liked, and the blue light on the cockpit flashed to indicate that five-foot-long probes, like curb feelers, on three of the four legs had touched the surface.

sentences.en: Finally, Mr. Armstrong found the spot he liked, and the blue light on the cockpit flashed to indicate that five-foot-long probes, like curb feelers, on three of the four legs had touched the surface.
sentences.zh: 最后，阿姆斯特朗先生找到了他喜欢的地方，驾驶舱上的蓝光闪烁，表明四条腿中的三条上的五英尺长的探针，就像路缘触角一样，已经接触到了表面。

blocks.html_en: "Contact light," Mr. Armstrong radioed.

sentences.en: "Contact light," Mr. Armstrong radioed.
sentences.zh: “接触灯，”阿姆斯特朗先生用无线电说道。

blocks.html_en: He pressed a button marked "Stop" and reported, "okay, engine stop."

sentences.en: He pressed a button marked "Stop" and reported, "okay, engine stop."
sentences.zh: 他按下了标有“停止”的按钮并报告说：“好的，发动机停止。”

blocks.html_en: There were a few more cryptic messages of functions performed.

sentences.en: There were a few more cryptic messages of functions performed.
sentences.zh: 还有一些关于所执行功能的更神秘的消息。

blocks.html_en: Then Maj. Charles M. Duke, the capsule communicator in the control room, radioed to the two astronauts:

sentences.en: Then Maj.
sentences.zh: 然后少校。

sentences.en: Charles M.
sentences.zh: 查尔斯·M.

sentences.en: Duke, the capsule communicator in the control room, radioed to the two astronauts:
sentences.zh: 控制室里的太空舱通讯员杜克用无线电向两名宇航员发送了信息：

blocks.html_en: "We copy you down, Eagle."

sentences.en: "We copy you down, Eagle."
sentences.zh: “我们抄袭你，老鹰。”

blocks.html_en: "Houston, Tranquility Base here. The Eagle has landed."

sentences.en: "Houston, Tranquility Base here.
sentences.zh: “休斯顿，这里是宁静基地。

sentences.en: The Eagle has landed."
sentences.zh: 老鹰已经着陆了。”

blocks.html_en: "Roger, Tranquility," Major Duke replied. "We copy you on the ground. You got a bunch of guys about to turn blue. We are breathing again. Thanks a lot."

sentences.en: "Roger, Tranquility," Major Duke replied.
sentences.zh: “收到，宁静，”杜克少校回答道。

sentences.en: "We copy you on the ground.
sentences.zh: “我们在地上模仿你。

sentences.en: You got a bunch of guys about to turn blue.
sentences.zh: 你们有一群人快要变蓝了。

sentences.en: We are breathing again.
sentences.zh: 我们又开始呼吸了。

sentences.en: Thanks a lot."
sentences.zh: 多谢。”

blocks.html_en: Colonel Aldrin assured Mission Control it was a "very smooth touchdown."

sentences.en: Colonel Aldrin assured Mission Control it was a "very smooth touchdown."
sentences.zh: 奥尔德林上校向任务控制中心保证这是一次“非常顺利的着陆”。

blocks.html_en: The Eagle came to rest at an angle of only about four and a half degrees. The angle could have been more than 30 degrees without threatening to tip the vehicle over.

sentences.en: The Eagle came to rest at an angle of only about four and a half degrees.
sentences.zh: 老鹰以大约四度半的角度停了下来。

sentences.en: The angle could have been more than 30 degrees without threatening to tip the vehicle over.
sentences.zh: 该角度可能超过 30 度，而不会造成车辆翻倒的危险。

blocks.html_en: The landing site, about 120 miles southwest of the crater Maskelyne, is on the right side of the moon as seen from earth. The position: Lat. 0.799 degrees N., Long. 23.46 degrees E.

sentences.en: The landing site, about 120 miles southwest of the crater Maskelyne, is on the right side of the moon as seen from earth.
sentences.zh: 着陆点位于马斯基林环形山西南约 120 英里处，从地球上看位于月球的右侧。

sentences.en: The position: Lat.
sentences.zh: 位置：纬度。

sentences.en: 0.799 degrees N., Long.
23.46 degrees E.

blocks.html_en: Although Mr. Armstrong is known as a man of few words, his heartbeats told of his excitement upon leading man's first landing on the moon.

sentences.en: Although Mr. Armstrong is known as a man of few words, his heartbeats told of his excitement upon leading man's first landing on the moon.
sentences.zh: 尽管阿姆斯特朗先生被认为是一个沉默寡言的人，但他的心跳却诉说着他在带领人类首次登陆月球时的兴奋之情。

blocks.html_en: At the time of the descent rocket ignition, his heartbeat rate registered 110 a minute--77 is normal for him--and it shot up to 156 at touchdown.

sentences.en: At the time of the descent rocket ignition, his heartbeat rate registered 110 a minute--77 is normal for him--and it shot up to 156 at touchdown.
sentences.zh: 在下降火箭点火时，他的心率为每分钟 110 次——77 次对他来说是正常的——而在着陆时心率飙升至 156 次。

blocks.html_en: At the time of the landing, Colonel Collins was riding the command ship Columbia about 65 miles overhead.

sentences.en: At the time of the landing, Colonel Collins was riding the command ship Columbia about 65 miles overhead.
sentences.zh: 着陆时，柯林斯上校正乘坐哥伦比亚号指挥舰，距离上空约 65 英里。

blocks.html_en: Mission control informed the colonel, "Eagle is at Tranquility.

sentences.en: Mission control informed the colonel, "Eagle is at Tranquility.
sentences.zh: 任务控制中心通知上校：“鹰号位于宁静岛。

blocks.html_en: "Yea, I heard the whole thing," Colonel Collins, the man who went so far but not all the way, replied. "Fantastic."

sentences.en: "Yea, I heard the whole thing," Colonel Collins, the man who went so far but not all the way, replied.
sentences.zh: “是的，我听到了整件事，”柯林斯上校回答道，他是那个走了那么远但没有走完全程的人。

sentences.en: "Fantastic."
sentences.zh: “极好的。”

blocks.html_en: When the Apollo astronauts landed on the Sea of Tranquility, the temperature at their touchdown site was about zero degrees Fahrenheit in the sunlight, even colder in the shade.

sentences.en: When the Apollo astronauts landed on the Sea of Tranquility, the temperature at their touchdown site was about zero degrees Fahrenheit in the sunlight, even colder in the shade.
sentences.zh: 当阿波罗号宇航员降落在宁静海时，着陆点的温度在阳光下约为零华氏度，在阴凉处甚至更冷。

blocks.html_en: During a lunar night, which lasts 14 earth days, temperatures plunge as low as 280 degrees below zero. Unlike earth, the moon, having no atmosphere to act as a blanket, is unable to retain any of the day's warmth during the night.

sentences.en: During a lunar night, which lasts 14 earth days, temperatures plunge as low as 280 degrees below zero.
sentences.zh: 在持续 14 个地球日的农历夜晚，气温可降至零下 280 度。

sentences.en: Unlike earth, the moon, having no atmosphere to act as a blanket, is unable to retain any of the day's warmth during the night.
sentences.zh: 与地球不同，月球没有大气层作为毯子，无法在夜间保留白天的任何温暖。

blocks.html_en: During the equally long lunar day, temperatures rise as high as 280 degrees. By the time of Eagle's departure from the moon, with the sun higher in the sky, the temperatures there will have risen to about 90 degrees.

sentences.en: During the equally long lunar day, temperatures rise as high as 280 degrees.
sentences.zh: 在同样长的农历白天，气温高达 280 度。

sentences.en: By the time of Eagle's departure from the moon, with the sun higher in the sky, the temperatures there will have risen to about 90 degrees.
sentences.zh: 当鹰号离开月球时，随着太阳在天空中升得更高，那里的温度将上升到约 90 度。

blocks.html_en: This particular landing site was one of five selected by Apollo project officials after analysis of pictures returned by the five Lunar Orbiter unmanned spacecraft.

sentences.en: This particular landing site was one of five selected by Apollo project officials after analysis of pictures returned by the five Lunar Orbiter unmanned spacecraft.
sentences.zh: 这个特殊的着陆点是阿波罗项目官员在分析了五艘月球轨道无人航天器返回的图片后选择的五个着陆点之一。

blocks.html_en: All five sites are situated across the lunar equator on the side of the moon always facing earth. Being on the equator reduces the maneuvering for the astronauts to get there. Being on the near side of the moon, of course, makes it possible to communicate with the explorers.

sentences.en: All five sites are situated across the lunar equator on the side of the moon always facing earth.
sentences.zh: 所有五个地点都位于月球赤道对面，月球始终面向地球的一侧。

sentences.en: Being on the equator reduces the maneuvering for the astronauts to get there.
sentences.zh: 在赤道上减少了宇航员到达那里的机动性。

sentences.en: Being on the near side of the moon, of course, makes it possible to communicate with the explorers.
sentences.zh: 当然，位于月球近侧可以与探险家进行交流。

blocks.html_en: Front Page Image Provided by UMI

sentences.en: Front Page Image Provided by UMI
sentences.zh: 首页图片由UMI提供

content_html_zh: 宇航员登陆平原；收集岩石，插上旗帜 约翰·诺贝尔·威尔福德 (John Noble Wilford) 仔细探索粉状表面 《纽约时报》特刊 相关头条新闻 月球之声：“老鹰已着陆” 月球航行：阿奇博尔德·麦克利什 (Archibald MacLeish) 报道 休斯敦，7 月 21 日星期一——人类已经登陆月球并在月球上行走。 昨天东部夏令时间下午 4:17:40，两名美国阿波罗 11 号宇航员安全平稳地驾驶着他们脆弱的四足登月舱，实现了历史性的着陆。 尼尔·A. 38 岁的文职指挥官阿姆斯特朗向地球和这里的任务控制室发送无线电：“休斯顿，这里是静海基地。 老鹰已经着陆了。” 第一个登上月球的人——阿姆斯特朗和他的副驾驶上校。 埃德温·E. 空军的小奥尔德林将他们的船停在干旱的静海西南岸附近一片岩石遍布的平坦平原上。 大约六个半小时后，阿姆斯特朗先生打开登陆艇的舱门，慢慢走下梯子，在月壳上留下第一个人类足迹时宣布：“这是人类的一小步，却是人类的一大步。” 他在晚上 10:56:20 迈出了月球的第一步，飞船外的电视摄像机将他的一举一动传送给了地球上数亿敬畏而兴奋的观众。 试探性步骤测试土壤阿姆斯特朗先生的最初步骤是试探性测试月球土壤的坚固性，以及他穿着笨重的白色宇航服和背包在月球重力（地球重力的六分之一）的影响下轻松移动的能力。 “表面很细，呈粉状，”宇航员报告道。 “我可以用脚趾松松地捡起它。 它确实像木炭粉一样细密地粘附在我靴子的鞋底和侧面。 我只进入了一小部分英寸，也许是八分之一英寸。 但我可以在细沙颗粒的胎面中看到靴子的脚印。 阿姆斯特朗先生进行了 19 分钟的测试后，奥尔德林上校在飞船外加入了他。 两人忙着从登月舱中安装另一台电视摄像机，将一面美国国旗插在地上，舀起土壤和岩石样本，进行科学实验，并在月球上跳跃和大步走来展示他们的敏捷性。 他们发现在月球上行走和工作比预期的要轻松得多。 阿姆斯特朗先生曾表示他“非常舒服”。 回到地球的人们发现，虫形登月舱和在其上行走的人们的黑白电视画面如此清晰，以至于看起来不真实，更像是一个玩具和玩具般的人物，而不是迄今为止进行的最大胆和影响最深远的探险中的人类。 尼克松致电祝贺 在宇航员工作的一次休息期间，尼克松总统在白宫向他们表示祝贺，他说，“这无疑是有史以来最具历史意义的电话”。 总统对宇航员说：“由于你们所做的一切，天空已成为人类世界的一部分。
